original_image_tags: A faded, light green paper document, likely the back of a folder or a routing slip. The paper shows signs of age and handling, with visible creases and two hole-punch marks at the top (one with a piece of tape). Multiple stamped impressions in blue, purple, and red ink are visible, oriented in different directions. Faint, illegible pencil writing appears on the right margin.

elements.description: A yellowed, mostly blank page, likely the back of a document. It contains several visible stamps and annotations. At the top, there are two punch holes. In the upper-middle area, a faded blue stamp reads 'RECEIVED' with a date of 'FEB 12 1950' and a number '80'. Below that, another stamp reads 'REL-FY' and 'FILE BY'. Near the bottom left, a larger blue stamp is visible, reading 'APR 12 11 40 AM '56' / 'RECEIVED - FILE ROOM' / 'F B I' / 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE'. In the bottom right corner, a red rectangular stamp is partially legible, showing a date of 'FEB 15 1950' and the word 'COLUMBIA'. Very faint, illegible text from the opposite side of the page shows through in several areas.

judge.reason: Description B provides specific quoted text for stamps that Description A only describes as 'multiple stamped impressions' without quoting, creating an omission rather than a contradiction.
